title: ugettxt
text: ugettxt Get the contents of a message or help text. Explain this code long ugettxt (unsigned char* id_string, char* buffer, long max); Copy Parameters id_string The ID string of the message or help text. buffer (Output) The contents of the message or help text max The maximum number of characters to be copied to the buffer pointed to by buffer . The size of the buffer should be at least max +1 to accommodate the zero terminator character. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ugettxt and ugettxtc Value Meaning >0 The number of bytes copied into the buffer 0 An error occurred <0 The negated number of bytes copied into the buffer. The negative status indicates that more data is available in the field, and that you should call ugettxtc to get additional data. Use Library: yrtl Description ugettxt moves the contents of the specified Uniface 4GL message or help text to the specified buffer. If there is more data available than the buffer can hold, ugettxt returns a negative status. In this case, you must call ugettxtc repeatedly to get additional data, until ugettxtc returns a positive status. Calling ugettxt and ugettxtc is functionally similar to using the ProcScript function $text( id_string ) . This function and its companion ugettxtc work very similar to the ufget / ufgetc pair. A notable difference is that there is no such thing as a bind name here. In fact, ugettxtc does not have a parameter for the ID string; it just reads from the same source as ugettxt does, so there would be no need for a bind name anyway. Note also that there is no parameter for the library that your message or help text resides in. You must specify this by setting $variation to the correct library in your application’s assignment file. Example: ugettxt and ugettxtc The following example retrieves and prints the string value of the message or help text whose IdString is contained in general register $1 . To illustrate the use of repeated ugettxtc calls, we read in small chunks and grow the buffer as necessary, in the same way as in the ufget / ufgetc example. Explain this code EXPORT void TEST_UGETTXT () { #define CHUNKSIZE 12 char id[51]; long len; char* buf; long totlen; ugetregs(1, (unsigned char *)id, sizeof(id)-1); buf = malloc(CHUNKSIZE); len = ugettxt(id, buf, CHUNKSIZE); totlen = abs(len); while ( len < 0 ) { buf = realloc(buf, totlen+CHUNKSIZE); len = ugettxtc(buf+totlen, CHUNKSIZE); totlen += abs(len); } buf = realloc(buf, totlen+1); buf[totlen] = 0; u_printf("The content of id %s is : %s\n", id, buf); } Copy Related Links ugettxtc Get additional data from a Uniface message or help text.
